items.title: Royal commission recommends national firearms agreement, buyback scheme

children.content: The Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion has released its interim report, making 14 recommendations.

articles.title: Counterterrorism intelligence's share of funding in 'significant' decline

children.content: Royal commissioner Virginia Bell says classified documents reveal a significant shift in intelligence spending away from counterterrorism since 2020, in the first findings of what failings led to last year's Bondi terror attack.

image.altText: Three men in corridor. Two wear darks suit, the third a police uniform.

articles.title: Government to implement all national recommendations of antisemitism interim report

children.content: The antisemitism royal commission releases its interim report, recommending an urgent review of Joint Counter Terrorism teams and greater police presence at high-risk Jewish events.  Follow live.

image.altText: Albanese speaks at a press conference

gallery.altText: Sam Mostyn holds a copy of the interim report.

thumbnail.altText: Sam Mostyn holds a copy of the interim report.

items.title: Christchurch mosque attacker's conviction appeal application declined by NZ court

children.content: A New Zealand court declines an application by the Australian man who murdered 51 people at two mosques in 2019 to appeal his convictions.

image.altText: A sign showing the Court of Appeal is displayed above a set of clear glass doors on a building.

items.title: Woolworths discounts not 'inherently misleading', judge says

children.content: There have been tense exchanges in the Federal Court, with the judge overseeing the bombshell case between the ACCC and Woolworths questioning the consumer watchdog's case.

image.altText: Close up of the green Woolworths W logo on the outside of a building.

items.title: Hegseth says Iran war is 'not a quagmire' as cost of conflict revealed

children.content: US Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth has defended the war against Iran in his first testimony to Congress since it started, instead attacking Democrats as "feckless". Follow live.

image.altText: Pete Hegseth with puckered lips, mid-speech, holding up two fingers on his left hand while sitting in congress.

items.title: Concerns for Sharon's survival as police confirm missing 5yo is non-verbal

children.content: Northern Territory Police Commissioner Martin Dole says he is still adamant there are people who have knowledge of the whereabouts of Jefferson Lewis, the man at the centre of a search for missing Alice Springs five-year-old Sharon Granites.

image.altText: Young girl in pink dress does peace sign with fingers

items.title: From cereal to clothing, see how the price of everyday items has changed

children.content: If it feels like everything is getting more expensive, that's because it is. With the rate of inflation rising this week, we've tracked how the price of everyday items has increased.

gallery.altText: A group of everyday items; coffee, light bulb, lemon with chart lines

thumbnail.altText: A group of everyday items; coffee, light bulb, lemon with chart lines

items.title: Accused double killer Beau Lamarre-Condon's mother to remain in custody

children.content: The mother of former police officer Beau Lamarre-Condon will spend at least two weeks in custody after she was charged over an alleged attempt to influence a key witness to change their evidence in his upcoming murder trial.

image.altText: Man in blue shirt smiling next to woman in white top

items.title: Mark Latham ordered to pay $100k to MP he sexually harassed and vilified

children.content: The former One Nation MP has been found to have vilified openly gay politician Alex Greenwich in the second case centring around a dispute between the pair.

image.altText: a composite image of Alex Greenwich and Mark Latham

items.title: Portrait of Jacob Collins wins Archibald Packing Room Prize

children.content: Sean Layh has won the $3,000 Packing Room Prize at this year's Archibald Prize for his portrait of Jacob Collins, titled The tragicall historie of Hamlet, Prince of Denmarke.

gallery.altText: Portrait of Jacob Collins by Sean Layh; a man laying in a disheveled bed.

thumbnail.altText: Portrait of Jacob Collins by Sean Layh; a man laying in a disheveled bed.

items.title: Coalition helps clear the way for One Nation in Farrer

children.content: Pauline Hanson is now set to tour more regional Coalition-held seats in her "sexy" new private plane, and the Liberal and National parties are helping clear the runway.

image.altText: Politician Pauline Hanson gestures to the camera

avatar.alt: David Speers sits on a couch in front of a yellow screen, smiling at the camera, in a suit and open-necked shirt.

items.title: Fuel shock pushes civil contractors to cut jobs and pause works

children.content: Civil construction firms warn surging diesel and material costs driven by the global oil crisis will force lay-offs and project delays, with nearly a third considering winding down.

image.altText: Diesel is pumped into a black ute

items.title: Has the world's most powerful cartel met its end?

children.content: A United Arab Emirates decision to pull the plug on its OPEC membership could be the nail in the coffin for an organisation that has struggled to keep its members in line for 66 years.

articles.title: ASX falls as price of oil surges to more than $US120 a barrel

children.content: The Australian share market has opened lower in morning trade as oil prices surge. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: ASX

articles.title: Australia's summer saw the dawn of the big battery, as gas drops to 25-year low

children.content: A hot summer saw Australians use record amounts of electricity, but the growing share of renewable energy continued to push fossil fuels out of the grid, driving gas generation to its lowest level in 25 years.

image.altText: Silhouette of transmission lines at dusk with sun flaring through

gallery.altText: A blue barrel bearing the logo of OPEC

thumbnail.altText: A blue barrel bearing the logo of OPEC

items.title: 'It's a safety issue': Construction industry faces gambling crisis

children.content: New research reveals 89 per cent of construction workers have gambled in the past year — higher than the national average.

image.altText: Men in high-viz and hard hats stand around building supplied chatting.

items.title: Lyon clarifies resignation offer over comment to First Nations players

children.content: St Kilda coach Ross Lyon is proud of the way the club reacted after he offended a group of the club's First Nations players.

image.altText: Ross Lyon looks to his left before an AFL match.

children.content: The latest inflation data is making headlines. Find out what it means for you in three minutes.

image.altText: An older woman in a jacket and glasses smiles

items.title: The 'Jessica' tantrum trend explained

children.content: Whether it's at home or in the middle of a shopping centre, tantrums can test the patience of any parent or caregiver.

image.altText: A man holding a small girl who is throwing herself backwards

items.title: Blues out to rewrite State of Origin history in Newcastle series opener

children.content: The Blues are yet to enjoy success in Newcastle, a fact they are mindful of ahead of the first Women's State of Origin match against the Maroons.

gallery.altText: A NSW Blues player holds the ball while being tackled by a Maroons opponent during the 2025 Women's State of Origin series.

items.title: Melbourne teacher charged after camera found in school bathroom

children.content: The 33-year-old was arrested at the school in the city's south-east after the discovery of a camera in a bathroom.

image.altText: A man wearing glasses

items.title: Keith Lees to stand trial over death of partner in 1997

children.content: Mr Lees will face a Supreme Court trial for allegedly killing Meaghan Louise Rose, whose death was originally suspected to be a suicide.

image.altText: Meaghan Louise Rose, who died in a fall at Point Cartwright in 1997, holding a scroll in an undated photo

items.title: Trump's would-be assassin took selfie 'armed to the teeth', court told

children.content: Court documents reveal further details about the case against the alleged gunman at the White House correspondents' dinner in Washington.

image.altText: Cole Tomas Allen taking a selfie in a mirror with various weapons on his person.

items.title: ATSB confirms student and instructor killed in Parafield plane crash

children.content: The ATSB said the plane had been doing circuit training at the airport, but it's not yet known if structured training was underway when it crashed.

image.altText: Black smoke billows from a plane crash at Parafield Airport

items.title: Fed-up residents 'leaving town' due to murky tap water

children.content: When residents of Narrandera, in southern NSW, turn on the tap, the water that comes out is sometimes so dirty it stains their clothes.

items.title: Melbourne rush-hour chaos as hundreds of rail passengers left stranded

children.content: A rail equipment fault caused havoc on the V/Line Geelong Line on Wednesday night, with rail operators warning of more delays today.

items.title: Why slick home appliances are actually harder for some to use

children.content: While the newest home technology aims to make life easier, when it prioritises aesthetics over usability, some lives are actually made much harder, writes Nas Campanella.

image.altText: A white woman's face is reflected in a shiny black microwave window

items.title: Just one rental in Australia affordable for JobSeekers like Jess

children.content: Anglicare Australia's latest look at rentals shows the private market effectively does not exist for poorer Australians.

image.altText: A woman with dark hair wearing a brown pantsuit stands in the doorway of a home.

items.title: ASIC drops case against Liberty Bell Bay smelter as buyer sought

children.content: It was alleged Liberty Bell Bay's former owner, British billionaire Sanjeev Gupta's GFG Alliance, had failed to lodge financial returns for numerous years. The company then went into administration, and ASIC has dropped its legal case.

image.altText: A blue sign with a flame on it and the word 'LIBERTY' underneath, on the side of the road.

items.title: Wine code of conduct details emerge as industry continues to shrink

children.content: Industry representatives welcome a new mandatory code of conduct that dictates how wineries treat wine grape growers, but it may be too late for some producers.

gallery.altText: Rows of grapevines lie in darkness as the yellow sun peeks over distant hills

thumbnail.altText: Rows of grapevines lie in darkness as the yellow sun peeks over distant hills

items.title: Israel's massive strike was opposite of surgical, Lebanon deputy PM says

children.content: Lebanese Deputy Prime Minister Tarek Mitri says the government cannot exercise its authority over the country while Israel continues its bombardments and occupation.

image.altText: Soldiers standing on top of tanks with a border wall in the distance and a green hill

items.title: Piastri ready to race again in Miami after F1's enforced break

children.content: Given a month-long enforced break, Australia's Formula 1 star Oscar Piastri is keen to regain lost momentum when racing resumes in Miami this weekend.

image.altText: Oscar Piuastri smiles and gives a thumbs up after qualifying in Japan

items.title: King visits 9/11 memorial as he tours New York

children.content: King Charles III and Queen Camilla met survivors and family members of victims, as well as with New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani.
